subject: English
text: A company of five thousand soldiers, having marched tirelessly for over five days, have just moved into their cantonment.
options: A company of five thousand soldiers,
having marched tirelessly for over five days,
have just moved into their cantonment.
explanation: The subject 'A company' is a collective noun and is singular. The verb should be 'has' instead of 'have' to maintain subject-verb agreement.

subject: English
text: Every person who believes in principles must stand up to fight for their convictions.
options: Every person who believes in principles
explanation: 'Every person' is singular. The possessive pronoun should be 'his/her' instead of the plural 'their'.

subject: English
text: The Olympic Games reflects the highest spirit of human endeavour and achievement.
explanation: 'Games' is plural, so the verb should be 'reflect' instead of 'reflects'.

subject: English
text: The principal of the school stressed the need for discipline amongst the students.
explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct.

subject: English
text: Failure is the stepping stone to success; however, successive failures are not successive stepping stones to success.
options: Failure is the stepping stone to success;
explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct.

subject: English
text: India's strengths are its diversity of culture and the spirit of tolerance in it's people.
options: the spirit of tolerance in it's people.
explanation: 'it's' is a contraction of 'it is'. The possessive form 'its' should be used instead.

subject: English
text: Once considered ninth planet of the solar system, Pluto is today listed as the largest dwarf planet of the solar system.
options: Once considered ninth planet of the solar system,
Pluto is today listed as the
largest dwarf planet of the solar system.
explanation: The definite article 'the' is required before the ordinal number 'ninth'.

subject: English
text: The greatest glory in life is to be able to realize ones dreams and ambitions without trampling on those of others.
options: is to be able to realize ones dreams and ambitions
without trampling on those of others.
explanation: The possessive form of 'one' is 'one's'.

subject: English
text: To be able to manage one's anger is a reflection of an individual's psychological maturity.
options: To be able to manage one's
anger is a reflection of an individual's
explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct.

subject: English
text: A honest mistake is no more than that; just an honest mistake.
explanation: The article 'an' should be used before 'honest' because the 'h' is silent and the word starts with a vowel sound.

subject: English
text: Counting your chickens
explanation: The idiom refers to being overly confident of success before it is certain.

subject: English
text: The icing on the cake
options: baked food that is delicate and delicious with a topping
extra benefit over and above an already good deal
explanation: It refers to an additional benefit that makes a good situation even better.

subject: English
text: A stitch in time
options: timely action that prevents a negative outcome
explanation: From the proverb 'a stitch in time saves nine,' meaning prompt action prevents future trouble.

subject: English
text: At sixes and sevens
explanation: This idiom describes a state of total confusion or disorder.

subject: English
text: Talking twenty to the dozen
explanation: It means to talk very quickly and continuously.

subject: English
text: Under the weather
explanation: If you are under the weather, you feel slightly ill or unwell.

subject: English
text: To sit on the fence
explanation: It means to avoid making a decision or choosing sides in an argument.

subject: English
text: Once in a blue moon
explanation: This refers to an event that happens very rarely.

subject: English
text: Through thick and thin
options: among people both fat and slim
explanation: It means to support someone or stay loyal through all circumstances, especially difficult ones.

subject: English
text: Like chalk and cheese
explanation: If two things are like chalk and cheese, they are completely different from each other.

subject: English
text: P: but perhaps the best way is to agree / Q: there are many ways of dealing / R: with them without excessive argumentation / S: with intransigent customers
explanation: The logical order is: 'There are many ways of dealing (Q) with intransigent customers (S) but perhaps the best way is to agree (P) with them without excessive argumentation (R).'.

subject: English
text: P: in the years following / Q: India achieved remarkable economic development / R: liberalization in the year 1991 / S: the landmark reforms inaugurated via
explanation: The logical order is: 'The landmark reforms inaugurated via (S) liberalization in the year 1991 (R) in the years following (P) India achieved remarkable economic development (Q).'.

subject: English
text: P: was the victory secured by the women's hockey team / Q: against Australia in the Tokyo Olympics / R: the greatest moment in Indian Olympic history / S: in the arena of sport, perhaps
explanation: The order is: 'In the arena of sport, perhaps (S) was the victory secured by the women's hockey team (P) against Australia in the Tokyo Olympics (Q) the greatest moment in Indian Olympic history (R).'.

subject: English
text: P: in terms of GDP and GNP, / Q: for gauging the success of a nation / R: which are the two major determinants / S: economic progress is determined
explanation: The order is: 'Economic progress is determined (S) in terms of GDP and GNP (P) which are the two major determinants (R) for gauging the success of a nation (Q).'.

subject: English
text: P: with the aplomb of a stage artist, / Q: that they are not men of straw / R: they can twist and shake their wobbly heads / S: mesmerizing the gullible into believing
explanation: The order is: 'They can twist and shake their wobbly heads (R) with the aplomb of a stage artist (P) mesmerizing the gullible into believing (S) that they are not men of straw (Q).'.

subject: English
text: P: of its rain-soaked verdure and on the other there are / Q: the artifacts of its visible modernity / R: on the one hand there is the immense beauty / S: [No unique part S in source, likely QRSP context]
explanation: The sequence is: 'On the one hand there is the immense beauty (R) of its rain-soaked verdure and on the other there are (P) the artifacts of its visible modernity (Q).'.

subject: English
text: P: are often rooted / Q: the causes / R: of extreme poverty / S: in the inequalities of social systems
explanation: The order is: 'The causes (Q) of extreme poverty (R) are often rooted (P) in the inequalities of social systems (S).'.

subject: English
text: P: a person by the way / Q: he behaves and / R: you must judge / S: not by the way he looks
explanation: The order is: 'You must judge (R) a person by the way (P) he behaves and (Q) not by the way he looks (S).'.

subject: English
text: P: to the greatest number of people / Q: whenever in doubt / R: that causes the least discomfort / S: always opt for the option
explanation: The order is: 'Whenever in doubt (Q) always opt for the option (S) that causes the least discomfort (R) to the greatest number of people (P).'.

subject: English
text: P: brought by bad times / Q: against the despondency / R: our greatest defence / S: faith and belief are
explanation: The order is: 'Faith and belief are (S) our greatest defence (R) against the despondency (Q) brought by bad times (P).'.

subject: English
text: He is essentially a crude person.
explanation: 'Crude' means lacking culture or refinement. 'Refined' is the opposite.

subject: English
text: His confidence is high.
explanation: 'Confidence' means self-assurance. 'Diffidence' means lack of self-confidence.

subject: English
text: His integrity is noticed.
explanation: 'Integrity' means the quality of being honest. 'Dishonesty' is the opposite.

subject: English
text: She is a benevolent individual.
explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind and well-meaning. 'Malevolent' means having a wish to do evil to others.

subject: English
text: His sartorial manner is judged.
explanation: 'Sartorial' relates to tailoring or clothes. 'Unstylish' is a logical opposite in the context of fashion.

subject: English
text: This piece of art is authentic.
explanation: 'Authentic' means of undisputed origin. 'Fake' means not genuine.

subject: English
text: The deluge affected the population.
explanation: 'Deluge' means a severe flood. 'Drought' means a prolonged period of low rainfall.

subject: English
text: His dedication is known to all.
explanation: 'Dedication' means being committed. 'Apathy' means lack of interest or concern.

subject: English
text: His perspicacity was remarkable.
explanation: 'Perspicacity' means the quality of having a ready insight into things. 'Dullness' is the lack of sharp insight.

subject: English
text: He has a penchant for spicy food.
explanation: 'Penchant' means a strong liking. 'Dislike' is the opposite.

subject: English
text: After a good meal, it is important to pay a compliment to the chef.
explanation: 'Compliment' means a polite expression of praise. 'Praise' is the nearest meaning.

subject: English
text: His work is laudable.
explanation: 'Laudable' means deserving praise. 'Praiseworthy' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: Raj is a competent carpenter.
explanation: 'Competent' means having the necessary ability. 'Capable' is the nearest meaning.

subject: English
text: He is diligent in submitting assignments.
explanation: 'Diligent' means showing care and effort. 'Conscientious' is the nearest meaning.

subject: English
text: He appears to be reticent.
explanation: 'Reticent' means not revealing one's thoughts readily. 'Silent' is the most appropriate synonym here.

subject: English
text: He is always jovial.
explanation: 'Jovial' means cheerful and friendly. 'Cheerful' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: Mohan remains morose these days.
explanation: 'Morose' means sullen and ill-tempered. 'Sullen' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: He was a reluctant learner.
explanation: 'Reluctant' means unwilling and hesitant. 'Unwilling' is synonymous.

subject: English
text: Amit is optimistic about the prospects of his investments.
explanation: 'Optimistic' means hopeful. 'Sanguine' also means optimistic or positive.

subject: English
text: Sachin is very cooperative by nature.
explanation: 'Cooperative' involves working together. 'Complaisant' means willing to please others or oblige.

subject: Physics
text: What is the mass of a material, whose specific heat capacity is $400~J/(kg~°C)$ for a rise in temperature from $15~°C$ to $25~°C$ when heat received is 20 kJ?
explanation: Using $Q = mc\Delta T$: $20000 = m \times 400 \times (25 - 15) \implies 20000 = 4000m \implies m = 5~kg$.

subject: Physics
text: The specific latent heat of vaporization of a substance is the quantity of heat needed to change unit mass from
options: liquid to vapour with a change of temperature
liquid to vapour without a change of temperature
vapour to liquid without a change of temperature
vapour to liquid with a change of temperature
explanation: Latent heat is the energy required to change state without changing temperature.

subject: Physics
text: Evaporation from the surface of a given liquid takes place more rapidly when
options: the temperature is high and the surface area of the liquid is large
the temperature is low and the surface area of the liquid is large
the temperature is low and the surface area of the liquid is small
the temperature is high and the surface area of the liquid is small
explanation: Evaporation rate increases with higher temperature and larger surface area.

subject: Physics
text: Which of the following statements correctly explains/explain the existence of a positive force between two electric charges? 1. Both the charges are positive. 2. Both the charges are negative. 3. Both the charges are oppositely charged.
explanation: A positive (repulsive) force exists between like charges (both positive or both negative).

subject: Physics
text: An electric wire of resistance 50 ohm is cut into five equal wires. These wires are then connected in parallel. What is the equivalent resistance of this combination?
explanation: Each piece has $R = 50/5 = 10~\Omega$. In parallel, $1/R_{eq} = 5 \times (1/10) \implies R_{eq} = 2~\Omega$.

subject: Physics
text: The electric field lines from an isolated positively charged conducting sphere are
options: at right angles to the conducting surface and towards the centre of the sphere
at any angle to the conducting surface
at right angles to the conducting surface and outwards from the centre of the sphere
explanation: Electric field lines are always perpendicular to the conductor surface and point away from positive charges.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is not a solution?
explanation: Milk is a colloid, not a true solution.

subject: Chemistry
text: Refining of petroleum is carried out using which one of the following techniques?
explanation: Petroleum is refined into various fractions using fractional distillation.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is a chemical change?
explanation: Milk turning sour involves a chemical reaction producing new substances, unlike physical changes like melting.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is the correct molecular formula of ammonium carbonate if the valency of ammonium ion is (+1) and carbonate anion is (-2)?
explanation: Valencies are crossed: $(NH_{4})^{+}$ and $(CO_{3})^{2-}$ result in $(NH_{4})_{2}CO_{3}$.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is a covalent compound?
explanation: Silicon carbide (SiC) is a network covalent compound.

subject: Chemistry
text: The mass number of argon is 40. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The number of protons in argon is 22.
The number of neutrons in argon is 18.
The number of electrons in argon is 18.
The sum of numbers of protons and electrons in argon is 40.
explanation: Argon has atomic number 18, so it has 18 protons and 18 electrons. Neutrons = Mass - Atomic No = $40 - 18 = 22$.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is the correct order of the valencies of elements Ne, Si, N and Mg?
explanation: Valencies: Ne = 0, Mg = 2, N = 3, Si = 4. Thus Ne < Mg < N < Si.

subject: Physics
text: The frequency of an alternating current is 3 Hz. It implies that
explanation: Hertz (Hz) is defined as cycles per second.

subject: Physics
text: Which one of the following correctly represents the SI unit of resistivity?
explanation: The SI unit of resistivity is Ohm-meter ($\Omega \cdot m$).

subject: Physics
text: What is the current required to light a 60 W incandescent bulb in a domestic supply of 240 V?

subject: Physics
text: The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire at a point outside the wire depends
options: inversely on the distance from it
directly on the distance from it
inversely at short distances and directly at large distances from it
directly on the distance (at short distances) and inversely on the distance (at long distances) from it
explanation: The magnetic field $B$ is inversely proportional to the distance $r$ ($B \propto 1/r$).

subject: Physics
text: What is the dimension of gravitational constant?

subject: Physics
text: A ball is thrown vertically upward with a speed of 40 m/s. The time taken by the ball to reach the maximum height would be approximately
explanation: Using $v = u + at$: $0 = 40 + (-10)t \implies t = 4~s$.

subject: Physics
text: The time period of a 1 m long pendulum approximates to
explanation: $T = 2\pi \sqrt{L/g} \approx 2\pi \sqrt{1/9.8} \approx 2~s$.

subject: Biology
text: Which of the following statements about living and non-living being is/are correct? 1. While living being can demonstrate growth and repair, non-living being cannot. 2. While living being demonstrates metabolic processes, non-living being does not.
explanation: Both growth/repair and metabolism are fundamental characteristics of living beings.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following plant plastids stores starch, oil and protein granules?
explanation: Leucoplasts are non-pigmented plastids that store nutrients like starch.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following statements about 'vacuoles' is not correct?
options: In plants, there is a large central vacuole that may occupy 90% of total cell volume.
In plant cells, vacuoles provide turgidity and rigidity.
In Amoeba, vacuoles have role in nutrition.
Vacuoles are absent in animal cells.
explanation: Vacuoles are present in animal cells, though they are usually smaller and more numerous than in plant cells.

subject: Biology
text: In aquatic plants, large air sacs give them buoyancy effects. These sacs are surrounded by which one of the following types of tissues?
explanation: The specialized parenchyma tissue in aquatic plants is called aerenchyma.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following belongs to 'Pisces'?
explanation: Dogfish is a true shark/fish. Jellyfish, Silverfish, and Starfish are not biologically classified as fish.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following groups is called 'amphibians of plant kingdom'?
explanation: Bryophytes are called amphibians of the plant kingdom because they live on land but need water for sexual reproduction.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following is caused by a bacterial pathogen?
explanation: Typhoid is caused by the bacterium Salmonella typhi. AIDS, Dengue, and COVID-19 are viral.

subject: Physics
text: The twinkling of a star is due to the atmospheric
explanation: Stars twinkle because their light is bent (refracted) multiple times by different layers of the Earth's atmosphere.

subject: Physics
text: A mass M is dragged by a pulley on a horizontal plane by a force anti-parallel to its displacement. The work done in pulling the mass M is
explanation: When force and displacement are anti-parallel (180 degrees), work done is negative ($W = Fd \cos 180 = -Fd$).

subject: Physics
text: A 5 N force is defined when a mass of 10 kg is accelerated with
explanation: Using $F = ma$: $5 = 10 \times a \implies a = 0.5~m/s^2$.

subject: Physics
text: A boy of mass 52 kg jumps with a horizontal velocity of 2 m/s onto a stationary cart of mass 3 kg. The cart is fixed with frictionless wheels. Which one of the following would be the speed of the cart?
explanation: By conservation of momentum: $m_1v_1 = (m_1+m_2)v$. So, $52 \times 2 = (52+3) \times v \implies 104 = 55v \implies v \approx 1.89~m/s$.

subject: Physics
text: The energy possessed by a body due to its change in position or shape is called
explanation: Potential energy is the energy held by an object because of its position relative to other objects or its configuration/shape.

subject: Physics
text: A sound wave has a frequency of 1 kHz and wavelength 50 cm. How long will it take to travel 1 km?
explanation: Speed $v = f \lambda = 1000 \times 0.5 = 500~m/s$. Time = distance/speed = $1000/500 = 2~s$.

subject: Chemistry
text: For an element with atomic number 35, which one of the following will be the correct number of electrons in its valence shell based on Bohr's model of an atom?
explanation: Configuration: 2, 8, 18, 7. The outer (valence) shell has 7 electrons.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is not an example of a redox reaction?
explanation: Option (a) is a double displacement/hydrolysis reaction where oxidation states do not change.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following allotropes of carbon is isomorphous with crystalline silicon?
explanation: Diamond has a tetrahedral crystal structure similar to that of crystalline silicon.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is the colour of hydrogen gas?
explanation: Hydrogen gas is a colourless, odourless, and tasteless gas.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following is not a pigment?
explanation: Silica is generally used as an extender or filler rather than a primary pigment.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following statements about fertilizers is not correct?
options: Application of fertilizer to the soil increases fertility of the soil.
Urea can be prepared by action of ammonia and carbon dioxide under high pressure and at high temperature.
Urea contains more nitrogen than other fertilizers.
explanation: Urea is a nitrogenous fertilizer, not phosphorus-containing.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Most carbon compounds are good conductors of electricity.
Bonding in organic compounds is covalent.
Graphite is used as a lubricant.
Diamond is an allotrope of carbon.
explanation: Most carbon compounds are poor conductors of electricity as they do not have free ions or electrons.

subject: Chemistry
text: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
explanation: The breakdown of AgCl into silver and chlorine by sunlight is a decomposition reaction.

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following is the mechanism of action of oral contraceptive pills?
options: They inhibit the release of egg.
explanation: Oral contraceptives primarily work by preventing ovulation (the release of an egg).

subject: Biology
text: Which one of the following plants has unisexual flowers?
explanation: Papaya is a well-known example of a plant with unisexual flowers.

subject: Biology
text: A cell is unable to synthesize lipids. Which of its cell organelles might be defective?
explanation: The Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) is responsible for lipid synthesis.

subject: Physics
text: All objects experience a buoyancy when they are immersed in a fluid. Buoyancy is
explanation: Buoyancy is the upward force exerted by a fluid on an immersed object.

subject: Physics
text: According to Fleming's right-hand rule, if the forefinger indicates the direction of magnetic field and thumb shows the direction of motion of conductor, then the stretched middle finger will predict the direction of
explanation: In Fleming's Right-Hand Rule, the middle finger represents the direction of induced current.

subject: Physics
text: Two resistors R1 and R2 arranged in parallel combination in an electrical closed circuit are made of the same material and of same thickness. If the length of R2 is twice the length of R1, then the total resistance R satisfies
explanation: Since $R \propto L$, $R_2 = 2R_1$. In parallel: $1/R = 1/R_1 + 1/R_2 = 1/R_1 + 1/2R_1 = 3/2R_1 \implies 3R = 2R_1$.

subject: Physics
text: What is the magnification produced by a concave lens of focal length 10 cm, when an image is formed at a distance of 5 cm from the lens?

subject: Physics
text: A wooden box of mass 2 kg and dimensions (30 cm x 15 cm x 10 cm) is placed on a table with sides 30 cm and 10 cm touching the tabletop. Which one of the following is the approximate pressure exerted on the table?
explanation: Pressure = Force/Area. Force $\approx 20 N$. Area = $0.3 \times 0.1 = 0.03 m^2$. $P = 20 / 0.03 = 666.6 N/m^2$.

subject: Physics
text: Why are the tyres of aircrafts made of conducting rubber? 1. So that the charge accumulated on the aircraft in flight, by rubbing the air, can easily be transferred to ground on landing. 2. So that the charge accumulated due to the operation of various electronic equipments in the aircraft in flight can easily be transferred to ground on landing.
explanation: Conducting rubber allows static charges from friction and electronics to dissipate safely into the ground.

subject: History
text: Arthashastra, the classical work of Indian political thought, focuses primarily on
explanation: Kautilya's Arthashastra is a comprehensive treatise on statecraft and administration.

subject: History
text: When did Charaka Samhita originate?
explanation: Charaka Samhita is believed to have been composed between the 3rd and 2nd centuries BCE.

subject: History
text: Early Buddhist sculptors did not show Buddha in human form. Through which of the following symbols was Buddha's presence shown by the early sculptors?
options: Empty Seat, Wheel, Tree and Stupa
explanation: Early aniconic Buddhist art used various symbols like the Bodhi tree, wheel, stupa, and empty seat.

subject: Polity
text: The Parliament of India is primarily based on the model of
explanation: The Indian parliamentary system is patterned after the British Westminster model.

subject: Polity
text: Who among the following had the shortest span in office as the Prime Minister of India?
explanation: Among the options, Chaudhary Charan Singh had the shortest tenure as PM.

subject: Polity
text: Which one of the following does not fall under the jurisdiction of the apex court of India?
explanation: Collegiate jurisdiction is not a recognized category for the Supreme Court.

subject: Geography
text: According to the Indian Meteorological Department, which of the following is/are the feature/features of cloudburst? 1. Heavy precipitation in short period of time in a limited geographical area 2. It occurs generally during monsoon period and triggers flash flood and landslides
explanation: Cloudbursts are intense local rainfall events often causing disasters during monsoons.

subject: Geography
text: In a group discussion on shape and size of the Earth, three students stated the following points: 1. Student 1: The shape of the Earth is basically an oblate spheroid. 2. Student 2: The polar diameter of the Earth is more than the equatorial diameter. 3. Student 3: Bulge along the equatorial region is due to revolution of the Earth. Who among the above students is/are correct?
explanation: Student 1 is correct. Student 2 is wrong (equatorial diameter is larger) and Student 3 is wrong (bulge is due to rotation, not revolution).

subject: Geography
text: Which of the following is/are required for you to be able to locate the place on the map?
options: Both longitude and latitude of the place
Latitude, longitude and altitude of the place
explanation: To locate a place on a flat map, both latitude and longitude coordinates are necessary.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following statements with respect to Global Positioning System (GPS) is not correct?
options: It is based on network of satellites orbiting above the Earth.
It is based on the system of triangulation.
GPS receivers provide location in terms of latitude, longitude and altitude.
It provides information exclusively for military operations.
explanation: GPS is available for both civilian and military use.

subject: Polity
text: Which one of the following is not a Constitutional Commission of India?
options: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes
The National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
The National Commission for Backward Classes
explanation: The National Commission for Women is a statutory body, not a constitutional one.

subject: History
text: Which Chinese traveller in ancient India wrote the diary called 'Records of the Travels to Middle India'?
explanation: Xuanzang is famous for his detailed travelogue of India.

subject: Polity
text: The elections to the Lower House of the Parliament of India, held in 2019, constituted the
explanation: The 2019 general elections were for the 17th Lok Sabha.

subject: Polity
text: Panchayati Raj Institutions are primarily the institutions of
explanation: Panchayati Raj systems are established to ensure local self-governance.

subject: History
text: Which of the following is/are the right/rights mentioned in the 'American Declaration of Independence'? 1. Life 2. Liberty 3. Pursuit of Happiness 4. Fraternity
explanation: The Declaration mentions 'Life, Liberty and the pursuit of Happiness'. Fraternity is from the French Revolution.

subject: History
text: Who among the following American Presidents described democracy as 'Government of the People, for the People and by the People'?
explanation: Abraham Lincoln famously used this phrase in his Gettysburg Address.

subject: Geography
text: Inhabitants are unaware of the speed of rotation of the planet Earth because 1. the angular velocity is constant for each place on the Earth's surface 2. the atmosphere rotates with the Earth 3. there are no nearby objects, either stationary or moving at a rate different from that of the Earth
explanation: All listed factors contribute to why we do not feel the Earth spinning.

subject: Geography
text: This has resulted due to which of the following processes? 1. Mass wasting 2. Erosion 3. Weathering
explanation: Weathering loosens the rock, and mass wasting (gravity-driven movement) brings it down.

subject: Geography
text: Which of the following statements in respect of landslides are correct? 1. These occur only on gentle slopes during rain. 2. They generally occur in clay-rich soil. 3. Earthquakes trigger landslides.
explanation: Landslides typically occur on steep slopes. Clay soil and earthquakes are common triggers.

subject: Geography
text: Consider the following features about a geographical phenomenon: 1. Torrents of snow and ice roaring down a steep mountain side 2. It is hazardous to skiers and mountaineers 3. It involves a mix of falling, rolling, sliding and flowing of materials. Which of the following phenomena can be identified with the above features?
explanation: The description perfectly matches an avalanche.

subject: History
text: The real beginning of western education in India can be dated from
options: the Macaulay's Minute on Indian Education, 1835
explanation: Macaulay's Minute of 1835 led to the adoption of English as the medium of instruction.

subject: History
text: The term 'Industrial Revolution' was first used by
explanation: Louis-Guillaume Otto and later J.A. Blanqui were among the first to use the term. Arnold Toynbee popularized it later.

subject: History
text: The Khudai Khidmatgar (Servants of the God) was organized by
explanation: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan founded this non-violent movement.

subject: History
text: The Mahad Satyagraha of 1927 was organized by
explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar led this satyagraha to allow untouchables to use water from public tanks.

subject: History
text: The Satyashodhak Samaj (Truth-Seeking Society) was set up by
explanation: Jyotiba Phule founded the Satyashodhak Samaj in 1873.

subject: History
text: The 13th Century text Lekhapaddhati gives us information on which one of the following topics?
explanation: Lekhapaddhati is a collection of models for legal and administrative documents.

subject: History
text: Nkrumah was one of the five leaders, who comprised the core of the Non-Aligned Movement (NAM). He was the leader of which country in Africa?
explanation: Kwame Nkrumah was the first President of Ghana and a key figure in NAM.

subject: Polity
text: The idea of Planning in Independent India was drawn from
options: the demand made by workers' unions
the Gandhian vision of India's future
explanation: The Bombay Plan of 1944 provided an early framework for state-led economic planning.

subject: Geography
text: Nisarga, Gati, Nivar, Tauktae and Yaas are names of
explanation: These are the names of various tropical cyclones that affected the Indian coasts.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following States/UTs was connected with the Indian Railways network in the year 2021?
explanation: Manipur's rail connectivity was significantly highlighted in 2021.

subject: Geography
text: The forests of Uttarakhand, Kullu Valley in Himachal Pradesh and Dzukou Valley in Nagaland and Manipur were in the news on account of which one of the following reasons?
explanation: These areas were severely affected by forest fires in early 2021.

subject: Polity
text: Which one of the following is correct in respect of total number of States and Union Territories in India?
explanation: Post-reorganization, India consists of 28 states and 8 UTs.

subject: Geography
text: Snow, sleet and hail are the forms of
explanation: Precipitation includes all forms of water falling from the atmosphere to Earth.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is likely to be the most prevalent form of weathering in hot-tropical desert areas?
explanation: Thermal expansion and contraction (mechanical weathering) is dominant in deserts.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following cities was the first to attain the status of a 'megacity'?
explanation: New York City is often cited as the first city to reach 10 million inhabitants.

subject: Geography
text: In India, rigid and stable elevated lands, denuded rocks and series of scarps are the features of which of the following?
explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is one of the oldest and most stable landmasses of India.

subject: Geography
text: A person had visited a region in India and found trees, such as Khair, Neem, Khejri and Palas. Which one of the following regions is she/he expected to have visited?
explanation: These tree species are typical of arid and semi-arid desert vegetation.

subject: Geography
text: Consider the following statements: 1. Rocks do not remain in their original form for long and undergo transformation. 2. Transformation of rocks is caused by weathering, erosion and metamorphic action. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation: Rocks are part of a continuous cycle and change form due to various geological processes.

subject: Geography
text: Weathering, mass wasting, erosion and transportation are indicators of which one of the following processes?
explanation: Denudation is the wearing away of the Earth's surface through these exogenetic processes.

subject: Geography
text: If you want to observe moraines, eskers and outwash plains, which one of the following Union Territories you may have to visit?
explanation: These are glacial landforms, which can be found in the glaciated regions of Ladakh.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Legendary Kathak dancer Pandit Birju Maharaj died recently. He was the doyen of which one of the following Gharanas of Kathak?
explanation: Pandit Birju Maharaj belonged to the Kalka-Bindadin Gharana of Lucknow.

subject: General Knowledge
text: In a first, India is to export BrahMos missile to
explanation: The Philippines was the first country to sign a deal for the BrahMos missile system.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Who among the following is the Head of the Committee appointed by the Supreme Court of India to enquire into the circumstances that led to the Prime Minister of India's convoy being stuck for several minutes on a flyover in Punjab recently?
explanation: Justice Indu Malhotra was appointed to lead the probe.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Who among the following retired from test cricket by bagging a wicket on the last ball of his career?
explanation: Ross Taylor famously took a wicket with the final ball of his Test career.

subject: General Knowledge
text: Who among the following is appointed as the tenth Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) recently?
explanation: S. Somanath succeeded K. Sivan as the Chairman of ISRO.

subject: Geography
text: According to the Forest Survey Report, 2021, area-wise which one of the following States has the largest forest cover in India?
explanation: Madhya Pradesh continues to have the largest forest cover by area.

subject: Polity
text: Who among the following is the Chairperson of the Goods and Services Tax Council?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
explanation: The Union Finance Minister chairs the GST Council.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has recently categorized the Red Sanders (Red Sandalwood) into
explanation: Red Sanders was recategorized as 'Endangered' by the IUCN.

subject: General Knowledge
text: The Government of India has decided to observe 'Veer Baal Diwas' on
explanation: December 26 is observed as Veer Baal Diwas to honor the martyred sons of Guru Gobind Singh.

subject: General Knowledge
text: On 14th January, 2022, which one of the following ministries has organized a global Surya Namaskar Demonstration programme?
options: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
explanation: The Ministry of AYUSH organized the large-scale demonstration on Makar Sankranti.
